data.title: Shell shares marked seven-session winning streak
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock moved 1% higher at $72.18 on Friday.

The oil and gas major gained nearly 6% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has advanced 14% so far this year, compared to a 2.4% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SHEL stock is up 7% over the past one month.

"Shell's management is more cautious, focusing on portfolio housekeeping and risk management rather than aggressive growth or bold bets," pointed out a Seeking Alpha analysis.

Shell will add as much as 12M metric tons of additional liquefied natural gas capacity by the end of the decade from projects under construction, according to a recent report.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SHEL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, B+ momentum, while it got a D+ growth factor. 

Turning to the Wall Street community, 15 analysts gave SHEL a Buy and above rating. Two analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while none recommended Sell or lower.

 
 
 
 
 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: New Fortress Energy plunges as creditors reportedly organize amid sliding bond prices
data.description:
New Fortress Energy (NASDAQ:NFE) -7.6% in Friday's trading as Bloomberg reports various creditor groups have have formed amid a mounting debt crisis at the liquefied natural gas company.

Some term loan lenders have organized with legal counsel Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld, while a group of creditors led by Capital Research Group is getting advice from Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton & Garrison, according to the report, which notes that the investors have holdings across the New Fortress (NASDAQ:NFE) debt structure.

The moves come as New Fortress (NFE) has struggled with delayed projects that have slowed its cash flow, and it is late in filing its Q1 report with the SEC, citing in part a delay in the $1B sale of Jamaican operations that was completed last month.

Some New Fortress (NFE) bonds have plunged to record lows below $0.50 on the dollar this month, Bloomberg reported, in the wake of recent downgrades by S&P Global Ratings and Fitch Ratings, with both citing the company's ability to refinance debt.

Meanwhile, doubts are rising about when operations will begin at New Fortress' (NFE) $1.1B LNG export project in northeastern Mexico, which is seen as crucial to the long-term future of the company.

With nearly $9B in debt, negative free cash flow and significantly lower operating profits than expected, New Fortress (NFE) may have to delay the start of the so-called FLNG-2 project, the credit ratings agencies said.

data.title: Beam reports positive results from sickle cell disease treatment
data.description:
Beam Therapeutics (NASDAQ:BEAM) shared promising results from the early stage trial of its genetically modified cell therapy, BEAM-101, for the treatment of severe sickle cell disease.

The data revealed that every patient who received BEAM-101 achieved significant improvements. It saw Hemoglobin F levels rise to over 60%, Hemoglobin S levels fall to less than 40%, and a complete resolution of anemia.

On average, patients required just one cycle of mobilization and showed quick recovery in neutrophil and platelet counts.

The safety profile of BEAM-101 appeared consistent with existing treatments, such as Busulfan conditioning and autologous hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, as well as with the underlying conditions related to sickle cell disease.

The company added that the enrollment for the trial has now concluded. By mid-2025, 30 patients are anticipated to have undergone dosing in the trial.

data.title: SEC makes several new hires for key division posts
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has filled several top division posts as it moves to bolster its leadership ranks, it said on Friday.

Jamie Selway has been tapped to serve as director of the Division of Trading and Markets starting June 17, 2025, the SEC said in a release, noting he was most recently a partner at Sophron Advisors, where he advised clients on capital markets issues.

The regulator also named Brian T. Daly as the new Director of the Division of Investment Management, effective July 8. For the past four years, he has been a partner in the investment management practice at Akin Gump Strauss Hauer & Feld LLP in New York.

Kurt Hohl, with almost four decades of accounting and auditing experience, has been appointed as chief accountant, effective July 7. He most recently founded Corallium Advisors, which helps businesses navigate the complexities of auditing, regulatory compliance, risk management and initial public offerings.

Also, the SEC is welcoming back Erik Hotmire as chief external affairs officer and director of the Office of Public Affairs, effective June 16.

data.title: BP poised to extend winning streak for seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Energy major BP (NYSE:BP) was gaining for the seventh straight session on Friday as its shares rose 2.33% to reach $31.64 during afternoon trading.

The company added more than 6% in the preceding six sessions. On a year-to-date basis, BP’s shares have gone up by 7% compared to a 2.39% rise in the S&P500 index.

Earlier this week, Bloomberg reported that Abu Dhabi National Oil Company is considering acquiring certain assets from the British oil firm, should the company choose to break itself up or face increased pressure to divest additional units. Adnoc has held initial consultations with bankers on the prospects, the report added.

BP’s market value has dropped by one-third in just over a year to under $80B and the firm has been struggling with extended underperformance.

Despite this, analysts are optimistic about the company. Both Seeking Alpha as well as Wall Street analysts have rated the stock as a Buy. However, as per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, it is advised to Hold the stock. The company has a score of 3.31 out of 5. It has been graded A+ for valuation but D for growth.

Seeking Alpha analyst Andrew Mackie recommends to Buy the stock. Mackie believes that the company is significantly undervalued compared to its U.S. peers, with a substantial valuation gap that he anticipates will narrow over the course of this decade.

“Cost reduction initiatives and portfolio rationalization should strengthen BP's balance sheet, despite recent challenges and activist pressure,” according to the analyst.

However, another analyst Long Player has issued a Sell call on BP arguing that the firm’s integrated strategy has long been hindered by a history of high costs and inefficiencies.

“This company has long struggled with cash generation and profitability. Right now, the current political situation threatens a trade war, tariffs, and a recession. This company, due to its issues, can be materially setback by a recession, whereas competitors bounce back much faster,” the analyst said in a recent article.

data.title: Measles cases in U.S. reach nearly 1,200
data.description:
• The number of confirmed measles cases in the U.S. has reached 1,197, according to the CDC.
  • Cases have been found in 34 states.
  • So far this year, 21 outbreaks have been reported, with 90% of cases being outbreak-associated. In 2024, there were 16 outbreaks.
  • There have been three deaths related to measles in 2025: Two in Texas and one in New Mexico.
  • The CDC recommends the two-dose MMR vaccine to prevent the disease and reduce the disease severity in people exposed to the virus. Merck (NYSE:MRK) and GSK (NYSE:GSK) market MMR vaccines in the U.S.

data.title: SA Asks: Which restaurant chains could be takeover targets?
data.description:
Papa John's (NASDAQ:PZZA) shares jumped as much as 15% on Wednesday amid reports that Apollo Management (APO) and a Qatari investment fund had made a potentially lucrative bid for the pizza chain. 

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Dr. Christopher Davis of Quad 7 and Pedro Goulart which restaurant chains could also be takeover targets.

Dr. Christopher Davis: The restaurant space is highly competitive with pretty thin margins and facing pressure from inflation, tariffs, and consumer confidence that has been shaky at best. This actually sets up a good environment for M&A in the space, particularly where there are beaten down shares/company valuations. Papa John's (NASDAQ:PZZA) had long been rumored to be a target. We think there are others that also could be targeted, but acknowledge this is speculation. 

We have long argued that Cava Group (NYSE:CAVA) would fit well with Chipotle (NYSE:CMG), just in terms of relatively "healthier and fresh offerings" on the menus, but this seems less and less likely to happen. 

There are some other names we also think are potentially ripe for M&A. The first is BJ's Restaurant (NASDAQ:BJRI), a bar/restaurant chain famous for its Pizookie offerings that has seen recent management changes. The stock has rallied of late, but on some weakness. We think the chain would make a good addition for a Darden (NYSE:DRI) or even a Brinker (NYSE:EAT) to diversify holdings. 

We also think you could see some consolidation in the coffee space. Again, very competitive, and Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS) has been in growth mode. The company looks ripe for a major coffee chain like Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) to come in and look to merge. 

Another name in fast casual that has been crushed is Red Robin Gourmet Burgers (NASDAQ:RRGB). Many years of sales and comps pressures. This one is a pretty good competitor with Brinker's (NYSE:EAT) fast casual offerings, and a strategic acquisition here could be in the cards. 

While there are balance sheet considerations for both potential targets and acquirers, we think these names are possibly up for grabs.

Pedro Goulart: A few restaurant chains are definitely in the spotlight this year, especially as organic growth gets harder to come by. Even with high capital costs, there are some interesting takeover talks floating around.

El Pollo Loco (NASDAQ:LOCO) is probably the clearest case. Biglari Holdings (NYSE:BH)—already known for owning Steak N’ Shake and Western Sizzlin’—seems to be eyeing a move into fast casual. They already have a 15% share, which makes things easier.

Another name that’s been popping up as a possible target is Nathan’s Famous (NASDAQ:NATH) — you know, the century-old hot dog legend. Smithfield Food Group (NASDAQ:SFD) might be interested, especially to tap into some operational synergies around product licensing. Plus, with the chairman hitting nearly 80 and holding about 25% of the company, it could be a pretty interesting exit opportunity.

One big name that's been struggling is Del Taco. The fast-food chain, owned by Jack in the Box (NASDAQ:JACK), has seen negative same-store sales for at least six straight quarters—and the drop actually sped up in Q1. Jack (NASDAQ:JACK) bought Del Taco for $585 million back in 2022, but now it's reportedly considering selling it off as part of its "Jack On Track" plan to cut $300 million in debt. Definitely one to watch.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Middle East tension, Paris Air Show, FOMC meeting, and triple-witching day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 16

  • Volatility watch -  Global stock markets will be on edge as developments in the Middle East play out. New Fortress Energy (NFE) and CoreWeave (CRWV) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include SafeSpace Global (OTCPK:SSGC), Klotho Neurosciences (KLTO), and M3-Brigade Acquisition V. (MBAV). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include PG&E (PCG) and Copart (CPRT).
  • IPO watch - Cancer diagnostics firm Caris Life Science (CAI) and Florida-based insurance firm Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE) are expected to price their IPO and begin trading.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Lennar (LEN).
  • Dividend watch - Some companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Iron Mountain (IRM), Meta Platforms (META), and Southwest Airlines (LUV).
  • All day - Amazon (AMZN) will hold its AWS re:Inforce conference in Philadelphia. The three-day event will provide the latest insights into securing workloads on AWS, including a keynote address from AWS Chief Information Security Officer Chris Betz. Shares of Amazon (AMZN) have rallied following the event in the past.
  • All day - The Paris Air Show will begin. The event will present an opportunity for aerospace companies to unveil new technologies, announce strategic orders, and reshape investor sentiment. Boeing (BA), Airbus (OTCPK:EADSY), Embraer (ERJ), Honeywell (HON), Eve Holdings (EVEX), GE Aerospace (GE) are some of the companies participating.
  • All day - The three-day Marine Money Week event will begin in New York City. The event is widely regarded as the largest annual gathering of the most active dealmakers in global ship finance.

 

Tuesday - June 17

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Jabil (JBL) and La-Z-Boy (LZB).
  • All day - GE Aerospace (GE) Chairman and CEO Larry Culp, CFO Rahul Ghai and other members of the senior leadership team will provide updates on the businesses and outlook.
  • All day - Shareholders with Penn Entertainment (PENN) will vote on whether to support three independent director candidates nominated by activist investor HG Vora Capital Management or stick with the board's preferred slate.
  • All day - Merck KGaA (OTCPK:MKGAF) will showcase its portfolio at the Silicon Saxony Day event.
  • 8:30 a.m. The U.S. Census Bureau will release the May Retail Sales report.
  • 9:00 a.m. Equifax (EFX) will host an Investor Day event featuring presentations from the senior leadership team on the company's EFX2027 strategic priorities and investing in new products, data, analytics, and EFX.
  • 1:00 p.m. Marvell Technology (MRVL) will host a major webinar titled "The Future of Custom Silicon Technology for AI Infrastructure."

 

Wednesday - June 18

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Korn/Ferry (KFY) and Smith & Wesson Brands (SWBI).
  • All day - The three-day MWC Shanghai conference will begin. The event will showcase the latest advancements in mobile technology, including 5G, artificial intelligence, the Internet of Things, robotics, and more.
  • All day - Myomo (MYO) will host its first-ever Investor & Analyst Day event.
  • 11:00 a.m. Pure Storage (PSTG) CEO Charlie Giancarlo will give a keynote speech at the company's Pure//Accelerate event.
  • 2:00 p.m. The FOMC will make its interest rate policy announcement.
  • 2:30 p.m. Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will hold a press conference.

 

Thursday - June 19

  • All day - The U.S. stock market is closed for observance of the Juneteenth federal holiday.

 

Friday - June 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Accenture (ACN), CarMax (KMX), Kroger (KR), and Darden Restaurants (DRI).
  • All day - The stock market could see extra volatility with triple witching day featuring the simultaneous expiration of stock options, stock index futures, and stock index options contracts.
  • All day - WTI crude July futures will expire. Crude oil futures have seen extra volatility over the last year on contract expiration dates.
  • 8:30 a.m. The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
  • Postmarket - FTSE Russell will release the last update to the additions and deletions for the Russell indexes. Companies may be added or removed from the Russell 3000, Russell 1000, or Russell 2000 based on their market capitalization and other eligibility.

data.title: Three out of three companies deliver EPS wins this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages on Friday pared back a chunk of their losses, though sentiment overall remained subdued after Israel attacked some of Iran's nuclear sites and military targets, raising fears of an all-out war in the Middle East.

Israel late on Thursday hit Iran with a series of what it called “preemptive” strikes. Iran retaliated by launching over 100 drones at Israel overnight. Israeli airspace has been closed, and a state of emergency has been declared amid expectations of another counterattack.

Amidst geopolitical tensions, all three out of the three S&P 500 companies that reported earnings this week, delivered EPS wins. 

Earnings Roundup: 

Let’s take a look at the quarterly performance of the companies that reported earnings this week. 

Adobe (ADBE)’s second quarter fiscal 2025 financial results easily surpassed expectations. The company also increased its guidance due to widespread adoption of its artificial intelligence offerings.

For the quarter ended May 30, the software giant reported adjusted earnings per share of $5.06, which was more than the consensus estimate of $4.97. It reported GAAP EPS of $3.94, which surpassed the estimate of $3.87.

J.M. Smucker (SJM) posted a mixed FQ4 earnings report. Sales were down 3% during the quarter to $2.1 billion. The 1% decrease in comparable net sales reflected a 3 percentage point decrease from volume/mix, primarily driven by decreases for dog snacks, sweet baked goods, lower contract manufacturing sales related to the divested pet food brands, and fruit spreads.

Oracle (ORCL) fiscal fourth-quarter results that topped estimates and offered a robust outlook for fiscal 2026.

For the period ending May 31, Oracle said it earned an adjusted $1.70 per share as revenue rose 11% year-over-year to $15.9B. Analysts expected the company to earn an adjusted $1.64 per share on $15.58B in revenue.

Earnings next week:

Around six companies are set to report earnings next week. These include Accenture (NYSE:ACN), Carmax (NYSE:KMX), Jabil (NYSE:JBL), Kroger (NYSE:KO), and Lennar Corp (NYSE:LEN).
